title: rubus obsectifolius
intro: Rubus obsectifolius, also known as the thimbleberry or false thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant is a shrub that produces edible fruits and has been traditionally used for its medicinal and nutritional properties. The medicinal properties of Rubus obsectifolius include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its fruits can be eaten fresh or used in jams, jellies, and other herbal preparations.
